headline: Private Hospital Costs Rise 45% While Public Hospitals Overflow
context: Malaysian Association of Private Hospitals reported that average treatment costs rose 45% between 2021 and 2025, pushing more patients into already overcrowded public hospitals where wait times for specialist appointments now average 4-6 months.

cards.big: Private hospital costs up 45% in 4 years — heart bypass now RM85,000 versus RM15,000 at public hospitals with 6-month wait
cards.sub: Two-tier healthcare means your wallet determines whether you wait 6 months or 6 days for treatment

cards.big: Government health spending at 2.3% of GDP is lowest in ASEAN — WHO recommends minimum 5% for universal coverage
cards.sub: Public hospitals serve 78% of population but receive only 55% of total national health expenditure

cards.big: 12,000 Malaysian doctors migrated overseas since 2020 — public hospitals now operate at 72% of needed specialist capacity
cards.sub: Average public hospital doctor sees 84 patients daily versus private hospital's 22 — burnout rate at 67%

cards.big: Private hospital fee schedules are self-regulated by industry — Health Ministry has advisory guidelines but no price enforcement power
cards.sub: Competition Commission investigated only 2 private hospital pricing complaints in 5 years despite 4,200 formal grievances

cards.big: Universal healthcare on paper means nothing when public hospitals are so overwhelmed that those who can afford it go private
Should Malaysia increase health spending to 4% of GDP and mandate private hospital fee caps for 50 common procedures?
